source.multipart_basis: Decomposition derived from Cal Poly CDS Rev 14 6U practice and GEVS GSFC-STD-7000 secondary-payload structures: rails + side panels + end decks + PC/104+ cage + deployable solar arrays with hinges are the canonical 6U bus breakdown. No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a 6U CubeSat primary structure benchmark for a constellation member, compliant with Cal Poly CDS Rev 14 for 6U deployers (12U / 24U capable P-PODs) and carrying deployable solar arrays. The structure is a skeletonized Al 7075-T6 chassis with external envelope 226.3 mm x 100.0 mm x 366.0 mm (6U standard), four deployer rails on the long 366.0 mm corners, and a total mass cap of 12.0 kg per CDS Rev 14 6U with 14 kg absolute cap. The structure must support two deployable solar arrays each 340 x 200 mm x 1.5 mm (triple-junction GaAs, 250 g each) folded against the long face during launch and deployed after separation to 45 degrees off-axis, plus an internal PC/104+ stack of 12 boards. Qualification loads per GEVS-SE GSFC-STD-7000 / NASA-STD-5001B: (LC1) quasi-static 15 g axial + 10 g lateral, (LC2) sine vibration 2.5 g at 5-100 Hz, (LC3) random vibration GRMS 14.1 at 20-2000 Hz, (LC4) shock 1000 g peak 100-10000 Hz, (LC5) thermal vacuum -40 to +70 C LEO thermal cycle. Material 7075-T6 (yield 503 MPa, E 71.7 GPa, density 2810 kg/m^3). Pass/fail: (R1) quasi-static combined load max von Mises must not exceed 251 MPa (yield 503 / SF 2.0), (R2) first natural frequency stowed must be at least 80 Hz per GEVS secondary payload, (R3) total mass at or below 12 kg, (R4) thermal cycle combined stress at hot case (+70 C) plus 15 g axial must not exceed 251 MPa, (R5) solar array tip deflection when deployed and operating at 45 degrees under 1g ground-test must not exceed 5 mm (reality check for hinge stiffness). Vibration (modal) is required for R2. Thermal is required for R4. Fluid and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly with chassis, solar array panels (stowed), hinges, and PC/104+ cage as sub-bodies plus a structural + thermal report.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This 6U bus is delivered as a multi-part assembly mirroring canonical 6U CubeSat practice: (A) four 366 mm corner rails, (B) a long-face panel pair and a short-face panel pair forming the four structural sides, (C) +Z and -Z end decks carrying the deployer interface and payload aperture, (D) an internal PC/104+ cage / midplate assembly locating twelve PCBs, (E) two deployable triple-junction GaAs solar-array panels stowed against the long face, (F) a set of solar-array root hinges and hold-down release mechanism (HDRM) brackets, and (G) the fastener set. FEM verification runs on the assembled model only; per-part stress is reported. All inherited load magnitudes (LC1-LC5), the 7075-T6 property set, and R1-R5 pass/fail thresholds are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria (R_asm1 connection DCR, R_asm2 HDRM release-path integrity, R_asm3 hinge-root stress under deployed 1 g ground-test) are added.
short_prompt:
Design a 6U CubeSat primary structure benchmark for a constellation member, compliant with Cal Poly CDS Rev 14 for 6U deployers and carrying deployable solar arrays.

The structure is a skeletonized Al 7075-T6 chassis with external envelope 226.3 mm x 100.0 mm x 366.0 mm, four deployer rails on the long 366.0 mm corners, and a total mass cap of 12.0 kg per CDS Rev 14 6U with 14 kg absolute cap. The structure supports two deployable solar arrays each 340 x 200 mm x 1.5 mm (triple-junction GaAs, 250 g each) folded against the long face during launch and deployed after separation to 45 degrees off-axis, plus an internal PC/104+ stack of 12 boards.

Material 7075-T6 (yield 503 MPa, E 71.7 GPa, density 2810 kg/m^3).

Load cases per GEVS-SE GSFC-STD-7000 / NASA-STD-5001B: LC1 quasi-static 15 g axial + 10 g lateral; LC2 sine vibration 2.5 g at 5-100 Hz; LC3 random vibration GRMS 14.1 at 20-2000 Hz; LC4 shock 1000 g peak 100-10000 Hz; LC5 thermal vacuum -40 to +70 C LEO thermal cycle.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Multi-sat benefit justification requires a constellation-level link and coverage budget, outside FEA.

prompt.title: 6U constellation CubeSat primary structure (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Bolted Al 7075-T6 6U CubeSat assembly (226.3 x 100 x 366 mm, <=12 kg): four corner rails, long/short side panels, +Z/-Z decks, PC/104+ cage, two deployable solar-array panels with hinges and HDRM, tied by fasteners. Must pass GEVS 15 g axial + 10 g lateral, modal >=80 Hz stowed, thermal -40/+70 C, and 1 g ground-deployment tip-deflection <= 5 mm at the assembled level.

parts.name: 6U corner rail
parts.role: primary load-path rail running the full 366 mm length in P-POD

parts.name: side panel (long 226.3 x 366 and short 100 x 366)
parts.role: structural side skin (two long-face, two short-face) closing the rail cage

parts.name: end deck (+Z / -Z)
parts.role: end plate closing the cage; hosts deployer interface and payload aperture

parts.name: PC/104+ internal cage / midplate set
parts.role: locates the 12-board PC/104+ stack on internal rails with standoffs

parts.name: deployable solar-array panel (stowed on long face)
parts.role: triple-junction GaAs array panel, stowed for launch, deployed to 45 deg off-axis
parts.material_hint: Al honeycomb / CFRP face sheet with GaAs cells

parts.name: solar-array root hinge + HDRM bracket set
parts.role: mounts solar array to long-face panel; includes spring-driven root hinge and hold-down release mechanism

parts.name: M3 / M4 fastener set
parts.role: bolts rails/panels/decks, PC/104+ cage to rails, hinges to panels

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: CDS Rev 14 6U: +Z along rail; long face is X-Z (226.3 x 366)

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across rail/panel/deck/PC104+ cage bolted interfaces <= 1.0 under governing launch load case (LC1 15 g axial + 10 g lateral).
HDRM release-path integrity: after LC3 random vibration the hold-down release mechanism must still actuate cleanly (no permanent yield in HDRM brackets, clearance >= 0.5 mm to array edge).
Solar-array root-hinge stress under deployed 1 g ground-test must remain below 251 MPa (7075-T6 yield/2) with tip deflection <= 5 mm.

interfaces.mediated_by: M3 SHCS bolted lap joint along full rail length
interfaces.load_path: corner rail (A) transfers shear into side panels (B) through bolt rows.

interfaces.mediated_by: M4 SHCS into rail end tapped holes
interfaces.load_path: rail (A) end reactions transferred into +Z/-Z end decks (C).
side panel (B) edge bolts to end deck (C) for torsional closure.

interfaces.mediated_by: Ti-6Al-4V standoffs bolted between rails and midplate
interfaces.load_path: PC/104+ cage (D) inertia under LC1/LC3 reacts through standoffs into rails.

interfaces.mediated_by: M4 SHCS bolt pattern on long-face panel
interfaces.load_path: solar-array root hinge / HDRM bracket (F) bolted to long-face panel (B); array inertia transfers into panel.
solar-array panel (E) hung on hinge (F); launch load reacted through HDRM hold-down points into brackets.

fem_check_policy.rationale: 6U compliance is defined at the integrated bus level including stowed solar arrays; individual panel stress only matters as a component within the assembled chassis with HDRM engaged.

notes.why_multi_part: 6U CubeSats with deployable arrays cannot be modeled as a single solid body: hinge stiffness, HDRM preload, and panel-rail joint compliance dominate first-mode behavior.
notes.speculation_caveats: Panel thicknesses, HDRM bracket count, and hinge detail are programmatic speculation within CDS 6U / GEVS practice.
